title: Private Lane Backup Governance Hardening Planning
objective: Define the bounded decision and planning surface for any future move from the current host-local snapshot, workstation-held export, workstation-mediated offsite mirror, and bounded daily automation posture into a stronger backup-governance shape for the host-only `personal-notes` private lane without making runtime, auth, ingress, or data-plane changes in this packet.
dependency_note: The bounded private personal lane is already operationally closed in host-only scope with validated host-local snapshots, workstation-held export, workstation-mediated OneDrive mirror, restore, and daily backup automation. The current authority surfaces still classify that posture as bounded private recovery rather than governed APEX backup posture. This packet exists to decide whether the next explicit Olares private-lane follow-on should be a stronger backup-governance path and, if so, what exact bounded implementation packet should open next.
required_inputs: The current private-lane runtime and recovery proof from ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-01-olares-private-stack-browser-terminal-bring-up-handoff.md
The bounded design and scope rules in docs/architecture/OLARES-PRIVATE-STACK-BLUEPRINT-2026-05-01.md
The first-run, backup, restore, and scheduler proof in docs/architecture/OLARES-PRIVATE-STACK-FIRST-RUN-CHECKLIST-2026-05-01.md and infra/private/README.md
The post-closure gating rules in docs/architecture/OLARES-POST-CLOSURE-EXECUTION-CHECKLIST-2026-04-25.md and plan/infrastructure-olares-full-implementation-roadmap-1.md
The bounded storage and offsite rerun authority in docs/architecture/OLARES-FIRST-STORAGE-BRING-UP-RUNBOOK-2026-04-23.md
A clear classification of whether the current workstation-mediated offsite mirror is sufficient or whether the service now deserves a stronger host-owned encrypted offsite posture
constrained_outputs: A bounded decision record describing whether the private lane should remain on the current bounded backup posture or move toward a stronger backup-governance shape
An explicit risk and validation checklist for the chosen backup-governance path
A follow-on implementation packet recommendation if and only if this planning decision approves one specific backup-hardening path
No runtime, ingress, auth, data, or deployment changes on the Olares host
validation_steps: Confirm the packet does not change the current host-only or mesh-tunneled private-lane runtime posture.
Confirm the packet does not add public ingress, shared-auth wiring, Olares app registration, or installed-app claims.
Confirm the packet preserves the rule that `forms-engine` and `p6-ingest` remain the only governed installed-app proof surfaces in the current snapshot.
Confirm any proposed next step is expressed as a new follow-on implementation packet rather than as direct runtime work in this planning packet.

Confirm the packet keeps current backup claims bounded to the tested host-local snapshot posture, workstation-held export, workstation-mediated offsite mirror, and bounded daily automation unless a later explicit backup-governance implementation packet changes that posture.
sql_permission_note: Planning only. This packet does not authorize SQL changes, database writes, ingress changes, auth changes, application registration, or host runtime mutation.
closure_note: This packet was executed on 2026-05-02 and concluded that the current bounded private-lane recovery posture remains truthful and operational, but that the next approved Olares private-lane follow-on should be stronger backup governance rather than public ingress, shared auth, or installed-app promotion. The specific approved next path is a separate implementation packet for host-owned encrypted offsite backup and restore-drill hardening, while keeping the existing workstation-mediated mirror as the interim floor until that later packet lands.
next_action: Author a separate implementation packet for host-owned encrypted offsite backup, retention, credential handling, and restore-drill hardening without widening auth, ingress, or installed-app scope.
